name: Maicas, Cortes de Aragón, and La Hoz de la Vieja Loop
name_original: Armillas - Hoz de la Vieja - Cortes de Aragón — circular desde Maicas
description: This moderate 28.1-mile mountain biking route from Maicas offers technical S2 terrain and scenic views in the Cortes de Aragón region.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Maicas traverse a landscape characterized by varied terrain, including valleys, rolling hills, and natural areas. The region offers diverse environments suitable for off-road cycling. Riders can expect routes that navigate through open spaces and along natural features, providing a range of experiences. The geological makeup of the area contributes to its distinct character, making it an engaging destination for mountain biking. Best mountain bike trails around Maicas The most popular mountain bike trail is Vuelta al Barranco Ancho, a difficult 39.1 miles (62.9 km) trail that takes 5 hours 44 minutes to complete. This route offers a challenging ride through varied natural landscapes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Maicas, Cortes de Aragón, and La Hoz de la Vieja Loop, a moderate 28.1 miles (45.3 km) path. This trail explores a mix of local towns and natural features. Local mountain bikers also love the Muniesa loop from Muniesa, a 14.7 miles (23.7 km) trail leading through local countryside, often completed in about 2 hours 6 minutes. Mountain biking around Maicas is defined by its valleys, natural areas, and varied terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more difficult, longer routes. The routes in Maicas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 45 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Maicas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Maicas?
A: Maicas offers a selection of 16 mountain bike trails, catering to various skill levels. You'll find options ranging from easy to difficult, ensuring there's a ride for every mountain biker.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when mountain biking in Maicas?
A: The mountain bike trails around Maicas traverse a diverse landscape, characterized by valleys, rolling hills, and natural areas. Riders can expect routes that navigate through open spaces, alpine meadows, and potentially dense forests, offering varied environments for off-road cycling. The region's geological makeup contributes to its distinct character, providing engaging and scenic rides.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Maicas?
A: Yes, Maicas has trails suitable for different abilities. There is 1 easy trail and 9 moderate trails, which can be a good starting point for less experienced riders or families looking for a more relaxed outing. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541464" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muniesa loop from Muniesa&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 14.7-mile trail that leads through local countryside.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Maicas?
A: The mountain bike routes in Maicas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain and the engaging natural landscapes that define the region's cycling experience.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge, Maicas offers 6 difficult trails. A notable option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1006865421/vuelta-al-barranco-ancho" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vuelta al Barranco Ancho&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 39.1-mile trail that typically takes nearly 6 hours to complete, offering a demanding ride through varied natural landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Maicas?
A: Yes, Maicas features several circular routes. A popular choice among local mountain bikers is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1006804653/maicas-cortes-de-aragon-and-la-hoz-de-la-vieja-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maicas, Cortes de Aragón, and La Hoz de la Vieja Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 28.1-mile path that explores a mix of local towns and natural features.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural features can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: While riding the mountain bike trails around Maicas, you can explore several interesting points. These include the natural area of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5143010" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoz de la Vieja&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4596234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santa Ana&lt;/a&gt;, and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5382837" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estrecho de la Parada&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass through charming settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/8027227" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huesa del Común&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5224874" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Segura de los Baños&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike routes available?
A: For those looking for longer rides, Maicas offers several extensive routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996262168/obon-alcaine-y-embalse-de-cueva-foradada-circular-desde-hoz-de-la-vieja" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Obón, Alcaine y Embalse de Cueva Foradada — circular desde Hoz de la Vieja&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 44.9-mile circular route, providing a significant endurance challenge. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1006847693/muniesa-huesa-del-comun" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muniesa - Huesa del Común&lt;/a&gt; trail, a difficult 38.4-mile route.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Maicas?
A: Given the region's characteristics, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking in Maicas, with milder temperatures and vibrant natural scenery. Summers can be warm, while winters might bring colder weather, potentially impacting trail conditions in higher or more exposed areas.

Q: Can I expect scenic viewpoints along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the mountainous terrain surrounding Maicas, similar to regions like Mackay, Idaho, suggests that many trails will offer stunning mountain vistas and panoramic views of valleys, expansive meadows, and natural features. The diverse ecosystems also provide opportunities for wildlife observation.

Q: Are there any specific trails that are particularly popular with local riders?
A: Local mountain bikers frequently enjoy the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1006804653/maicas-cortes-de-aragon-and-la-hoz-de-la-vieja-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Maicas, Cortes de Aragón, and La Hoz de la Vieja Loop&lt;/a&gt;, as well as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541464" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Muniesa loop from Muniesa&lt;/a&gt;. These routes are well-regarded for their blend of natural beauty and engaging riding experiences.
tips: Nice views of the town of Maicas from the town cemetery. Maicas is a town in the Cuencas Mineras region and has about 38 inhabitants (INE 2008).
Beautiful picture that we are at the top of the hill located in the Sierra de Cortes, near the Cabezo Redondo or the town of Hoz de la Vieja. A nice stop on our route.

faqs.question: How long does it take to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The Armillas - Hoz de la Vieja - Cortes de Aragón — circular desde Maicas mountain biking route is estimated to take approximately 4 hours and 12 minutes to complete. This duration is for the full 45.3 km circular trail.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is classified as moderate and is not suitable for beginners. It has an STS S2 rating, meaning you'll encounter larger roots, stones, occasional steps, loose surfaces, narrow curves, and gradients up to 70%. It requires precise braking, good center of gravity control, and overall advanced riding skills.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Armillas - Hoz de la Vieja - Cortes de Aragón route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and predominantly off-road, aligning with an STS S2 rating. Expect natural paths with obstacles like roots and rocks, sections with loose surfaces, narrow turns, and some steep ascents and descents. Riders should be prepared for a technically engaging ride.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or landmarks along the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers scenic views. You'll encounter the View of Maicas and the View of Sierra de Cortes. The trail also passes through the 'Hoz de la Vieja', a deep, narrow valley, suggesting picturesque riverine landscapes within the natural Aragonese scenery.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead in Maicas?
faqs.answer: As a circular route starting from Maicas, you can typically find parking within the village itself. Specific designated mountain bike parking areas are not detailed, but local parking options in Maicas would serve as the trailhead access.

faqs.question: Is the Armillas - Hoz de la Vieja - Cortes de Aragón trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this particular trail are not provided, generally, mountain biking trails in natural areas often permit dogs. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash for their safety and to protect local wildlife, and to carry waste bags.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee for this trail or Espacio BTT Cuencas Mineras?
faqs.answer: This trail is located within the Espacio BTT Cuencas Mineras. Generally, access to natural mountain biking trails in Spain, especially those within designated BTT (Bicicleta Todo Terreno) centers, does not require a specific permit or entrance fee for individual riders. However, always check local regulations or the official Espacio BTT Cuencas Mineras website for any updates or specific rules regarding the use of their facilities.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to mountain bike this route would generally be during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers in Aragon can be very hot, and winters might bring colder weather or even snow at higher elevations, making the trail less enjoyable or potentially impassable in parts.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. You will ride along parts of the Camino a Armillas, Camino Cantalobos, Ferrocarril Zaragoza-Utrillas, and Pista a cantera during your ride.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation near Maicas?
faqs.answer: Maicas is a small village, so while specific amenities like cafes or dedicated accommodation directly at the trailhead might be limited, you can usually find basic services in nearby larger towns within the Cortes de Aragón region. It's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks for your ride.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the Armillas - Hoz de la Vieja - Cortes de Aragón route?
faqs.answer: You can expect to ride through the natural beauty of the Cortes de Aragón region in Aragon, Spain. The trail name 'Hoz de la Vieja' suggests deep, narrow river valleys, and the broader area is known for its green mountains and picturesque routes, offering classic Aragonese landscapes.
